body.title: [sprint] Ticket-close: scoped stash check on own agent:ticket: tag (surface, not block)
body.description:
I intend that closing a ticket surfaces — never silently leaves — a dangling stash that belongs to THAT agent and THAT ticket.

On `cc_queue.py` close of T-x by agent A, grep `git stash list` for the scoped tag `A:T-x:` only. If a match exists, surface it loudly (warning + a system_alarm naming the dangling stash) — the agent is closing a ticket with its own WIP still parked. It does NOT hard-block the close (surface-never-block): the scoping makes it safe to act on (it is the closing agent's own stash, never a sibling's), but a hard gate adds friction for a recoverable situation. Multi-agent-safe by construction: A's close never inspects or touches B's `B:T-y:` stashes.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (close path, near _proof_gate ~L998); NEW tests/test_stash_close_check.py.
**Design rules:** surface-never-block (a close gate could disrupt legit flow); scoped-by-attribution (depends on the agent:ticket: format from T-git-stash-attribution-wrapper); system_alarm canonical name.
**Scope boundary:** IN — at close, detect same-agent same-ticket dangling stash, surface (warn + system_alarm). OUT — hard-blocking the close; touching other agents'/tickets' stashes; the wrapper; the broad orphan sweep (separate ticket).
**Test plan:** tests/test_stash_close_check.py — (1) plant `A:T-x:WIP` stash, close T-x as A → warning + one system_alarm; (2) close T-x as A with no matching stash → silent; (3) plant `B:T-y:WIP`, close T-x as A → silent (no false positive on sibling tag). Proof: red where close ignores a same-tag dangling stash → green where it surfaces it.
**Completion criteria:** close of T-x by A surfaces (warn + alarm) iff an `A:T-x:` stash exists; never fires on a sibling agent/ticket tag; test red->green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that closing a ticket surfaces — never silently leaves — a dangling stash that belongs to that same agent and that same ticket.
